article_title: ACLU-Led Coalition of 75 Groups Demands Meta Abandon 'Name Tag' Facial Recognition Before It Ships on Ray-Ban Glasses

content_preview.title: ACLU-Led Coalition of 75 Groups Demands Meta Abandon 'Name Tag' Facial Recognition Before It Ships on Ray-Ban Glasses
content_preview.summary: A 75-organization coalition is pressing Meta to permanently drop plans for a face-identifying feature on its AI glasses, calling it 'a red line society must not cross.'
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A coalition of 75 civil liberties, privacy, and advocacy organizations led by the American Civil Liberties Union is urging Meta to immediately halt and publicly disavow plans to add facial recognition to its Ray-Ban and Oakley smart glasses, according to an [ACLU press release](https://www.aclu.org/press-releases/aclu-and-75-organizations-sound-alarm-on-metas-plans-to-add-facial-recognition-technology-to-ray-ban-and-oakley-eyeglasses) published April 13. The letter characterizes the...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong privacy/civil liberties article on ACLU coalition opposing Meta facial recognition glasses.
editor_notes.source_verification: ACLU press release VERIFIED via WebFetch: April 13 2026 letter, 75 organizations, ACLU/ACLU-MA/NYCLU lead, 'red line society must not cross' quote, 'Your glasses should not know my name' quote, Meta internal docs re dynamic political environment, $7B+ privacy settlements context all confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All coalition details and quotes verified.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral; reflects advocacy group concerns without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No prior ACLU-Meta glasses facial recognition coverage.
